items.title: Show HN: Orchestrate parallel Claude Code and Codex agents on a live map
items.why_it_matters: A developer shipped a Next.js tool that visualizes parallel Claude Code and Codex agent runs on a live map by reading your local session logs. If you run multiple agent sessions, this solves the headache of losing track of which subagent is doing what or which one quietly failed. You can clone the repo and run it locally this week to visually track your agents while they build AI-ingest.

items.title: Show HN: An always-fresh memory that learns your repo, so agents stop re-reading
items.why_it_matters: An open-source Claude Code plugin and MCP server called Live-Memory was released to act as a persistent memory of your codebase. It passively learns your repo's structure using a low-cost model, preventing your primary agent from constantly re-reading files and burning through your token budget. You can install this MCP server today to speed up your Claude Code sessions and lower your API costs.

items.title: Show HN: A little cat that counts your tokens (Claude and codex)
items.why_it_matters: A developer built a macOS menu bar app that tracks your real-time Claude and Codex token spend by reading your local log files. Instead of constantly refreshing developer dashboards or running CLI status commands, you get a clean, passive visual of your exact API usage. You can download the app today to monitor your token burn rate while vibe-coding.

items.title: Show HN: Open-source Claude Code skills that mine Reddit/X for content topics
items.why_it_matters: An open-source Claude Code skill pack was released that allows your agent to mine Reddit and X for trending content topics. This is a direct way to feed real-world user pain points into your development workflow without manually browsing social media. You can install this skill pack this week and have Claude Code analyze Reddit to find features your AI-ingest users actually want.
